programmes.show_description: When Mad Micky goes on the run to protest his innocence, Terry has to look after him and Arthur sees his chance of a scoop. S3 E4: Looking For Micky
With the aid of a jacket full of battle ribbons and Else's chair, Alf and Arthur find themselves in the money. S2 E5
Larry gives girls fake names; the most recent one he uses is Robin Tripp, so Chrissy and Jo think Robin is a real bounder when a girl turns up saying she is pregnant. S4 E4: Never Give Your Real Name
The two couples go to see a bullfight to see El Macho in action - David is fascinated, but the fight doesn't live up to his expectations. S1 E2: El Macho
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S4, E4: When Love Walks In
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S4, E5: An Open-And-Shut Case
The train journey along the West Cumbrian coast leaving Barrow-in-Furness, past the nuclear plant at Sellafield and taking us to Whitehaven has some stunning scenery which belies its industrial past.
Jet Hawk is cordless, rechargeable and turns any water source into a powerful pressure washer. Call or visit highstreettv.com for more details. Teleshopping.

Arthur digs a large hole for himself when he recruits some ex-cons as cheap labour for a landscape gardening enterprise amid a spate of local burglaries. S6 E1: Give Us This Day Arthur Daley's Bread
The terrible news about Lady Marjorie begins to affect everybody at Eaton Place - as Richard waits for news, an expected visitor calls late at night shocking all downstairs. S3 E2: A House Divided
Aveline's wedding is on again, Shifty starts a greengrocery business from his hearse and Mrs Boswell's new friendship brings a few surprises. S5 E12
The Steptoes go for their weekly visit to the cinema; unfortunately, their tastes in big screen entertainment differ wildly. S3 E5: Sunday For Seven Days
The staff are up early to greet a new arrival at Hatley Station - another arrival later in the day is less welcome... S1 E7: Past Love
When Miss Jones sees a prowler through her window, Rigsby suspects everyone else as a police detective appears and begins to investigate. S1 E6: The Prowler
When Chrissy's mother comes to stay, Jo and Chrissy try to hide the fact that their new flatmate is a man. S1 E2: And Mother Makes Four
The coast is clear for Gary to have a holiday with Phoebe, since Yvonne has packed her bags for a trip to Korea. But Gary also has to deal with an unexpected visitor. S4 E3: Out of Town
Jack looks after a socialist lecturer who is speaking at the Co-Op Hall, and Billy takes Bill to a New Year's party at Jessie's against his will. S1 E11: Happy New Year, Some Say
Arthur digs a large hole for himself when he recruits some ex-cons as cheap labour for a landscape gardening enterprise amid a spate of local burglaries. S6 E1: Give Us This Day Arthur Daley's Bread
Bittersweet sitcom. Penny and Vince go to a wedding as Daphne is subjected to a goodwill visit from the Pinner clan. S2 E4: Caught on a Shoulder Strap
Chrissy is practising for her driving test and is receiving lessons from Robin; meanwhile, George has started making home brew liquor which turns out stronger than expected. S4 E2: One for the Road
Classic sitcom featuring the accident-prone Frank Spencer and his long-suffering wife. Frank's attempt at building Jessica a doll's house in woodwork class gets out of hand. S3 E2: Wendy House
When Tracey has enough of Dorien entertaining gentlemen callers and Sharon not bringing in a living wage, she throws them both out and they end up in Sharon's old bedsit. S12 E4: Going, Going, Gone
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
A sophisticated antiques dealer turns up at the yard and Harold is very impressed, but Albert thinks he's after more than antiques. S5 E3: Any Old Iron
Mr Spooner annoys the household by playing his radio loudly - Rigsby is annoyed, but he finds it difficult to pluck up the courage to confront him about it. S1 E5: All Our Yesterdays
According to Bob and Shakespeare, 'wine is a gentle stimulant and good companion' - and so is Terry on the night Bob gets breathalysed. S2 E4: One for the Road
With typical aplomb, Arthur announces the launch of his Daley Into Space business, to flog some satellite dishes.
While Loretta takes Jodie back to the UK for her advert shoot, Rob tries to make use of her 5-star room, but the rest of the family have their own plans for its use. S9 E4

Yvonne gets a shock when she's confronted by a ghost from her past, and the consequences aren't worth thinking about. Desperate to keep the truth from Chesney and Meena, Yvonne must think fast. S6 E14
George has been claiming for a non-existent son for the past 19 years in order to fiddle his tax returns, but he is in danger of being caught when a tax inspector visits. S4 E6: My Son, My Son
Rigsby is dressed to kill as he takes Miss Jones out to dinner to celebrate her birthday. S1 E3: A Night Out
While Loretta takes Jodie back to the UK for her advert shoot, Rob tries to make use of her 5-star room, but the rest of the family have their own plans for its use. S9 E4
Terry finally gets the chance to taste the high life when he has to mind a singer's dream home - Arthur, as usual, has other ideas. S3 E5: Dreamhouse
An unexpected visit coincides with Andy and Fergie's Royal Wedding. Alf Garnett has rivals for his affections. In Sickness and In Health S2 E6
Robin accompanies Chrissy to her nephew's christening while Jo runs out of excuses not to hear George sing in the British Legion's choir concert. S4 E5: The Tender Trap
David is carried away by the throb of guitars, the drone of the cicadas, the scent of the bougainvillaea, and most of all by Linda. S1 E3: Praying Mantis
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Hit '70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, who are forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S5, E1: Make Yourself at Home
'70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, who are forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S5, E2: Compliments of the Season
The Ffestiniog Railway runs from the harbour at Porthmadog to the slate mining town of Blaenau Ffestiniog and takes in the breath-taking scenery of Snowdonia National Park.

A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Arthur gets into the car phone business not realising he is dealing with a dodgy supplier, and Terry fancies a girl who tries to hide her family background. S6 E2: Life in the Fast Food Lane
Hudson and James spend a weekend at Somerby Park that allows them to reflect on Eaton Place and how much they enjoy their home. S3 E3: A Change of Scene
Mr and Mrs Boswell pass on some heartfelt advice to their beloved daughter Aveline before she walks down the aisle. S5 E13
Cash flow problems cause Harold to buy some lead at a knockdown price from a stranger who visits the yard, but panic sets in when the police pay a visit. S3 E3: The Lead Man Cometh
May is keen to make a career move to a bigger station, but both Jack and Cecil are set against her going and both try to apply for a transfer to be near her. S1 E8: Job Opportunity
An election polarises the household - Rigsby supports the Conservatives, Alan and Philip lean towards Labour, and Miss Jones is impressed with the Liberal candidate. S1 E7: Stand Up and Be Counted
Robin and Chrissy return home to find their front door open and the rent money that was on the sideboard missing. S1 E5: It's Only Money
Ron needs Gary's help in court after he is arrested. In 1942, Phoebe reveals her husband has been killed in action, leaving her free to marry Gary. S4 E4: And Mother Came Too
Jack helps Tom take on two ruffians who threaten him over money, and Jessie tries to persuade a widow to let her son take up a scholarship. S1 E12: Heads You Win, Tails I Lose
Arthur gets into the car phone business not realising he is dealing with a dodgy supplier, and Terry fancies a girl who tries to hide her family background. S6 E2: Life in the Fast Food Lane
Bittersweet sitcom. Penny and Vince go to a wedding as Daphne is subjected to a goodwill visit from the Pinner clan. S2 E4: Caught on a Shoulder Strap
When suspected woodworm is discovered in the upstairs flat, the girls and Robin have to find alternative accommodation while George calls in his so-called handyman. S4 E3: All In The Game
Classic sitcom. When Betty comes home after a dance lesson with their neighbour, Frank becomes suspicious of his intentions and insists on getting involved. S3 E3: Scottish Dancing

Auntie Vera has got problems with her hips and her defective lifts and tells Sharon and Tracey she is moving in - there's only one room available, though. S12 E5: Tonight I'll Be Staying Here With You
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
A heavily pregnant young woman turns up looking for Harold who she thinks is the father; he warms to the idea of becoming a dad, but it can only end in pain. S5 E4: Steptoe and Son - and Son!
When Miss Jones sees a prowler through her window, Rigsby suspects everyone else as a police detective appears and begins to investigate. S1 E6: The Prowler
Bob and Terry argue about fitness over a pint and challenge each other to a cross-country cycle race to see who's the fittest. S2 E5: The Great Race
The Winchester Club is faced with permanent closure when one of Arthur's money-making schemes backfires badly, but Ray has an idea, a charity football match. S9 E9: Last Orders at the Winchester
Joyce asks Sir Henry to step in while she is at the conference in Barcelona, and Les has asked Neville from the Geordie Bar to cover for him. S9 E5
After managing to upset everyone, Frank gets cast out for the night by Debbie. He seeks shelter in a confessional booth, where he overhears a neighbour's shocking confession. S6 E15
It is Chrissy's birthday when she receives a cine camera and projector from her boyfriend Neil, but Larry plans to use it to watch blue movies. S5 E1: The Last Picture Show
Rigsby is given some advice from Alan and Philip - as well as some tablets - to calm his nerves in his amorous pursuit of Miss Jones. S1 E4: Charisma
